name: RS232ByteCallback
title: RS232ByteCallback
tables: Deprecated Function Replaced by RS232Receive and RS232OnReceive.
RS232ByteCallback( dword port, dword datum, dword note )

Callback handler for reception of data from serial ports. Each node which implements this handler will receive data. The handler receives data from all opened ports (i.e. opened by CANoe/CANalyzer). It is cumbersome and slower by design since it gets one byte time by time. Block handlers receive blocks of data at an instant of time.
port A number between 1 and 255 identifying a serial port.
datum Byte which has been received (lowest 8 bits).
note Constant 1 (for sake of interface compatibility).
0: error The error occurs if no serial port has been opened.

Example if ( 0!=RS232WriteByte(2,65) ) write(“Written byte to port 2.”);// port 2 may be connected with port 1...// at node which listens to port 1 is connected to port 2RS232ByteCallback(dword port, dword datum, dword note){ // receive value 65 with port==1}
